source: Rocky Linux Admin Guide, ch. 6.4 Inserting text (pp. 88-89)

questions.q: Which key inserts text BEFORE the current character, and which inserts AFTER it?
options: i inserts before the character; a inserts after it (append)
a inserts before; i inserts after
I inserts before; A inserts after
o inserts before; O inserts after
questions.why: i = insert (before the cursor character), a = append (after it).

questions.q: Which key starts inserting at the BEGINNING of the current line, and which at the END?
options: I inserts at the start of the line; A appends at the end of the line
i inserts at the start; a at the end
A inserts at the start; I at the end
O inserts at the start; o at the end
questions.why: Uppercase I jumps to the line's first character to insert, uppercase A appends at the line's end.

questions.q: Which key opens a NEW line BELOW the current line (and enters insert mode), and which opens one ABOVE?
options: o opens a line after (below); O opens a line before (above)
O opens a line after (below); o opens a line before (above)
a opens a line after; A opens a line before
i opens a line after; I opens a line before
questions.why: Lowercase o opens a line after the current one, uppercase O opens a line before it.

questions.q: After you finish typing in insert mode, how do you get back to command mode?
options: Press Esc
Press Enter
Press i again
Press : then Enter
questions.why: The insert keys switch VI into insert mode, so Esc is needed to return to command mode.

questions.q: You want to add text at the very end of the current line. Which single key is the most direct?
options: A (append at end of line)
a (append after the character)
i (insert before the character)
o (open a line below)
questions.why: Uppercase A goes straight to the end of the line and enters insert mode there.
